calendar.matters:
Order To Show Cause
Off Calendar Jun-12-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER CYPRESS SECURITY, L.L.C.'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE.
APPEARANCES: JOHN FOUST, ESQUIRE (THELEN REID, 415.369.7205) APPEARING FOR PETITIONER. OWEN J. CLEMENTS, ESQUIRE (OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3944) APPEARING FOR RESPONDENT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: The writ is denied. The Court reviews the matter under an abuse of discretion standard. The bid of Guardsmark substantially complies with the RFP. The Guardsmark bid with three alternative illustrative proposals was consistent with the RFP because the hourly rate did not change. Both Guardsmark and Cypress exceeded the page limitation and included attachments consistent with the DHS representation that was acceptable. Neither party complied with financial documentation requirement and both parties demonstrated the financial capacity to provide the services. The review panel followed the criteria in the RFP and price was only one factor. Assuming Cypress was entitled to additional points for price and/or that DHS considered higher compensation when that factor was not set forth in the RFP, DHS had the discretion to make the award where other delineated factors were favorable to Guardsmark. R&A Vending Services, Inc. v. City of Los Angeles (1985) 172 Cal.App.3d 1188, 1193; Monterey Mechanical Co. v. Sacramento Regional County Sanitation District (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 1391.
Finally, the Board of Supervisors considered several factors including the testimony of plaintiff's representatives that explained plaintiff's position on the cost analysis. The record does not support the conclusion that price was primary factor; it was one among other factors presented to the Board. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: CARL SAUCEDA, CSR 2031
